subject: UX & Research
title: The Clipboard: Copy, Cut, Paste
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Clipboard: Copy, Cut, Paste usable.

hook.text: Larry Tesler at Xerox PARC invented copy and paste in 1974. He wore a license plate that read 'NO MODES.'

buildup.text: Before cut and paste, moving text meant retyping it. Tesler's invention saved millions of hours of human labor.

discovery.text: The clipboard metaphor mimicked physical cutting with scissors and pasting with glue. Instantly understandable.

twist.text: Tesler hated modal interfaces where keys do different things in different states. He championed modeless design.

climax.text: Ctrl+C and Ctrl+V became the most used shortcuts in computing. Tesler's idea runs billions of times daily.

punchline.text: Copy and paste changed computing forever. One idea, billions of daily uses.

quiz.question: What design philosophy did Larry Tesler champion alongside copy-paste?
options: Modeless design — the same action should work the same way regardless of mode
Minimalist visual design with fewer colors
